statement.p: An object was launched from the top of a hill with an upward vertical velocity of 100 feet per second. The height of the object can be modeled by the function h(t)={-16t^{2}+100t+100}\text{,} where t represents the number of seconds after the launch. Assume the object landed on the ground at sea level. Find the answer using graphing technology.
The objects height was when it was launched.

solution.p: Use technology to graph the function h(t)={-16t^{2}+100t+100}\text{,} and then find the functions y-intercept, (0,100)\text{.}
According to the graphs y-intercept of (0,100)\text{,} the object was 100 feet high when it was launched.
